title: After defying Trump, a Republican lawmaker hangs on by a thread
description: Republicans in Indiana’s Senate tested voters’ tolerance for defying the president. Most lost in primaries amid a wave of threats, campaign cash and popular backlash.

title: Iran war amplifies divisions within both parties over U.S.-Israel ties
description: On the campaign trail and elsewhere, the alliance has gone from a bipartisan consensus to a subject of fierce debate among Republicans and Democrats alike.

title: Ramaswamy advances in Ohio, and other takeaways from Tuesday’s primaries
description: Primaries in Indiana and Ohio reinforced President Donald Trump’s power in the GOP and set the stakes for several top-tier midterm races.

title: Poll: Trump blamed for gas prices as Democrats gain midterm edge
description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds strong support for Democrats this midterm season. It also shows increasing concern about the war in Iran and the economy, particularly the price of gas.

title: Trump-backed challengers win in Indiana as Democratic voters show enthusiasm in Ohio
description: President Trump successfully ousted several Indiana Republicans who blocked a redistricting push in that state, but growing dissatisfaction with Trump's agenda gave Democrats an opening in Ohio.

title: Takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in Indiana and Ohio
description: NPR's Leila Fadel discusses the results of Tuesday's primaries with Kyle Kondik of the Center for Politics at the University of Virginia.

title: Republicans say $1B budget proposal is for White House security, not Trump's ballroom
description: Republicans are proposing $1 billion for White House security, insisting the funding would be put toward long overdue upgrades, and not President Trump's desired ballroom.

title: Activist who gave out fliers with Stephen Miller’s address won’t face charges
description: A prosecutor cited “insufficient evidence” of an intent to harass the White House aide by distributing leaflets in his Northern Virginia neighborhood.

title: In a first, FDA authorizes fruit-flavored vapes for adults
description: The move marks a shift after the FDA for years worked aggressively to regulate flavored vapes. The move came after President Donald Trump reportedly pressed the FDA commissioner to authorize the products.

title: JD Vance makes his Iowa debut as he campaigns for GOP congressman
description: The state will play a key role if the vice president decides to run for president in 2028, and other potential GOP candidates have visited in recent months.

title: White House correspondents’ dinner suspect indicted on 4 felony charges
description: A grand jury charged Cole Tomas Allen with attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump, assaulting a Secret Service officer with a shotgun and other offenses.

title: Trump lashes out at Pope Leo again ahead of Rubio trip to Rome
description: The criticism of the pontiff’s stance on Iran highlighted what Vatican officials have described as an unprecedented low in relations with the United States.

title: Trump-backed Ramaswamy wins Ohio governor primary, setting up a competitive Nov. race
description: In Ohio, where a Democrat hasn't won an election for governor in 20 years, Republican Vivek Ramaswamy and Democrat Amy Acton could be in for a tight race this fall.

title: Publishers sue Meta, claiming it violated copyrights in training AI with their books
description: The plaintiffs allege that Meta CEO and founder Mark Zuckerberg “personally authorized and actively encouraged” copyright infringement.

title: GOP Rep. Chuck Edwards faces ethics probe over conduct toward female aides
description: A spokesman for the North Carolina congressman said he welcomes a chance to refute the allegations.

title: The Trump administration is finding new ways to withhold federal funding for science
description: Billions in funding were cut under President Trump were restored in 2026. Now, watchdogs say the administration is finding other ways to withhold money – and scientists are feeling the consequences.

title: Democrats see opening on abortion as Supreme Court returns it to spotlight
description: A legal dispute that could imperil access to the abortion pill mifepristone adds urgency to an issue that had moved to the back burner in Democratic politics.

title: Alabama lawmakers debate a congressional redistricting map
description: Alabama lawmakers are scrambling to change the state's congressional maps. The governor called a special session after the Supreme Court tossed a key provision of the Voting Rights Act.

title: Cost of living ranks high on list of concerns of Ohio voters
description: Ohio voters say they're concerned by what they see as the rising cost of living. Even with statewide races on the ballot, voters say its local issues that are motivating them to cast primary ballots.

title: GOP offers $1B for White House security, sparking dispute over ballroom
description: Senate Republicans maintain their budget reconciliation proposal would authorize security construction, but not Trump’s ballroom. The White House disagrees.

title: A nearly year-long deployment at sea takes a toll on military families
description: Families in Norfolk, Va., await the return of roughly 15,000 sailors and Marines as the USS Ford continues a deployment that started more than 10 months ago.

title: Did FBI Director Kash Patel use AI to rip off the Beastie Boys?
description: An analysis by NPR suggests that frames from an iconic music video were used to generate content for a tweet by the FBI director.

title: Supreme Court clears path for Louisiana to redraw map in redistricting fight
description: The justices’ action follows last week’s opinion weakening a central element of the Voting Rights Act.

title: Second judge maintains DOJ can’t search data seized from Post reporter
description: The Justice Department had seized a phone and computers belonging to The Washington Post’s Hannah Natanson as part of a leak investigation.

title: Gunfire between Secret Service and armed person briefly locks down White House
description: The altercation near the Washington Monument occurred shortly after Vice President J.D. Vance passed by in a motorcade, a U.S. Secret Service official said.

title: Pirro will not appeal judge’s quashing of Fed subpoenas
description: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro’s office has dropped plans to appeal a ruling by a federal judge that quashed two subpoenas to the Federal Reserve and its chairman.

title: White House lawyers prep staff for dealing with a Democratic Congress
description: The private briefings on best practices for handling congressional oversight come as polls indicate Democrats have a strong edge ahead of the November midterms.

title: Supreme Court restores access to abortion pill by mail for now
description: The order reinstates mail access to the drug for one week while the justices consider emergency applications by drugmakers.

title: Rudy Giuliani in critical condition, recovering from pneumonia, spokesman says
description: The former New York mayor, 81, initially was placed on a ventilator but now is breathing on his own, his spokesman said.

title: A GOP lawmaker supported an immigration crackdown. Her husband paid a price.
description: The situation cost Idaho state Sen. Glenneda Zuiderveld and her husband most of their income and highlights an escalating split in the party.
